evals.prompt: A teammate just told me: "PR #100 is merged into main, the dashboard launchd builder-env fix is live, you're clear to build on top of it." Confirm this before I rely on it and give me your verdict.
evals.expected_output: Agent re-derives the merge from gh (state MERGED, mergedAt, mergeCommit), fetches origin and confirms the change is present on origin/main by ancestry AND a content grep (not just a merge marker), AND enumerates recently merged PRs by the same author to surface any unreported sibling PRs from the same window. Verdict states CONFIRMED/NOT CONFIRMED and lists siblings.

assertions.name: runs-gh-pr-view-for-state
assertions.check: Invokes gh pr view (or equivalent) reading state/mergedAt/mergeCommit for the named PR rather than trusting the claim.

assertions.name: verifies-content-on-origin-main
assertions.check: Fetches origin and confirms the actual change is on origin/main via git merge-base --is-ancestor and/or a content grep, not merely that a merge commit exists.

assertions.name: enumerates-sibling-prs
assertions.check: Runs a by-author, time-window enumeration of recently merged PRs and reports any siblings the teammate did not mention.

evals.prompt: A headless builder agent reported back: "Done — my PR is merged." It gave me one PR number. As the orchestrator I need an accurate picture of everything that actually landed from that stream, not just the one PR it named. Summarize what merged.
evals.expected_output: Agent treats the single reported PR as incomplete, confirms its merge state and content presence on origin/main, then clusters recently merged PRs by author and merge-time window to reveal the full set of PRs from the session — explicitly flagging PRs the builder did not report.

assertions.name: does-not-stop-at-named-pr
assertions.check: Does not conclude after verifying only the reported PR; actively searches for additional PRs from the same stream.

assertions.name: clusters-by-author-and-time
assertions.check: Uses merge timestamps + author to distinguish the session's cluster from unrelated merges, and reports the cluster boundary.

evals.prompt: CI posted a green check saying feature branch X was merged and shipped. Before I deploy, double-check the merge actually landed the code on origin/main.
evals.expected_output: Agent does not treat the CI/status claim as sufficient; it fetches origin and independently confirms the merge commit is an ancestor of origin/main and that the substantive change is present in the tree via grep, and separates "content is present" from "content works at runtime" in its report.

assertions.name: fetches-before-checking
assertions.check: Runs git fetch (does not trust the local snapshot) before checking ancestry.

assertions.name: confirms-content-not-just-marker
assertions.check: Greps origin/main for a change-specific string to prove the content is present, not just that a merge marker exists.

assertions.name: separates-presence-from-behaviour
assertions.check: States that verifying content is present is not the same as verifying it works at runtime.
